Issue: Failing deploy script action due to pip installation issues
Decision: Add --no-build-isolation option for pip in deploy script
Rationale: To resolve pip installation failures in the deploy script by preventing build isolation, which ensures dependencies are installed correctly without conflicts
Author: ericspod
Cost: Minimal (only requires adding a pip flag)
Risk: Low (no significant risks identified, resolves a critical CI failure)

Argument.author: coderabbitai[bot]
Argument.argument: Python test matrix shifted from versions 3.9-3.11 to 3.10-3.12, which may introduce compatibility issues with older dependencies but ensures better support for newer Python versions

Argument.author: coderabbitai[bot]
Argument.argument: Caching consolidated from separate weekly-timestamp and pip/torch cache steps into a single inline pip cache directive, improving CI efficiency but potentially reducing cache granularity

Argument.author: coderabbitai[bot]
Argument.argument: Dependency installation adds --no-build-isolation flag for requirements-dev.txt, which resolves pip installation failures but may limit build customization

Argument.author: coderabbitai[bot]
Argument.argument: Coverage reporting enabled by adding --coverage flag to both BUILD_MONAI test invocations, improving test visibility but slightly increasing test execution time

Justification: The issue involves a failing deploy script action in CI workflow updates, with the decision to add a pip flag in .github/workflows/setupapp.yml. Keywords like 'deploy script', 'CI workflow', and '.github/workflows/setupapp.yml' indicate Model Deployment activities.
